title: Microsorum subnormale
taxonomy: Microsorum subnormale is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, which is a group of ferns. This plant is a member of the genus Microsorum, which is commonly known as the 'prayer plant' or 'java fern' in some species. Microsorum subnormale specifically belongs to the subgenus Microsorum, which is native to tropical regions in Asia. The species is further classified into various infra-specific categories based on its morphological characteristics and geographic distribution. Within the Polypodiaceae family, Microsorum subnormale shares similarities with other closely related species, such as Microsorum pteropus.
uses: Microsorum subnormale has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to possess compounds with therapeutic benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. The plant is said to be used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and infections.
